lectures.text: The gradient collects the partial derivatives of a scalar function. Gradient descent uses the gradient to update parameters. A Taylor remainder describes the error left after truncating a Taylor expansion. Taylor remainders can be compared with gradient descent when studying approximation error in iterative methods.

An optimisation problem asks for an input that minimizes an objective. Gradients are applied in differentiable optimisation problems, and gradient descent is a method applied to such problems. Approximation remainders can bound errors in optimisation calculations.
